result.summary: Coralogix exposes authenticated management HTTP APIs, a hosted Grafana HTTP API, a streamable-HTTP MCP server, and the cx CLI; docs indicate API key auth broadly and OAuth/browser-login support for MCP and CLI.
result.description: Coralogix is an observability platform for logs, metrics, traces, security, and related operational data. It provides cloud-hosted tooling for ingesting, querying, visualizing, and managing observability data and platform configuration.

coralogix_api_key.label: Coralogix personal or team API key
coralogix_api_key.setup: In Coralogix, create a personal or team API key from the Data Flow area as described in [Getting started with Coralogix APIs](https://coralogix.com/docs/developer-portal/apis/getting-started/getting-started-with-coralogix-apis/). Choose the needed permission preset or custom permissions; many API pages note that personal or team API keys are accepted.

coralogix_oauth_token.label: Coralogix OAuth browser-login token
coralogix_oauth_token.setup: Use a Coralogix first-party OAuth browser login where supported. For the CLI, run the interactive login flow described in [CX CLI configuration](https://coralogix.com/docs/external/cx-cli/documents/configuration/). For the MCP server, connect via the OAuth flow described in [MCP setup](https://coralogix.com/docs/user-guides/mcp-server/setup/) and [MCP OAuth](https://coralogix.com/docs/user-guides/mcp-server/oauth/).

requiredHeaders.name: mcp-version
requiredHeaders.description: Shown in the MCP setup examples for Claude/Cursor registration.

variables.name: your-coralogix-domain
variables.description: Your Coralogix domain/region hostname, inserted into the API host as shown in the setup docs.
